approved_exception_note: No non-retrieval tooling edits are approved for this handoff; split tooling work into a separate lane.
kickoff_budget_note: Within the default AGENTS.md low-risk budget: 6 completed tasks, retrieval-owned paths only, no shared or integrator-locked edits, and required local gates rerun before handoff. The reviewed implementation commit set is explicit, the metadata-only follow-up commit is not the feature payload, and the regression test is part of the correction bundle.
roadmap_items: Milestone 3: Product Readiness -> generation provenance contract (retrieval evidence attached to outputs)
Milestone 4: Retrieval Layer -> FTS-first ingestion/index path for context/vault documents
scope_completed: The handoff now names the reviewed implementation commit set separately from the metadata-only follow-up commit, so the audit trail no longer implies that the follow-up commit is feature payload. PageIndex and embeddings are explicitly deferred fallback-only plumbing, so the FTS-first MVP remains the only required retrieval path. The correction also adds regression coverage for the canonical FTS excerpt lookup entrypoints.
scope_goal: Document the retrieval handoff boundary while keeping the lane-owned retrieval scope constrained to the reviewed implementation commit set and excluding out-of-lane tooling edits.
tasks_completed: Added an explicit reviewed commit-set field so the implementation bundle is auditable.
Separated the metadata-only follow-up commit from the feature payload.
Kept the reviewed implementation files listed separately for traceability.
Kept the handoff correction within the lane-owned retrieval documentation artifacts.
Documented PageIndex and embeddings as deferred fallback-only plumbing so the FTS-first MVP remains the only required retrieval path.
Added regression coverage for the canonical FTS excerpt lookup entrypoints and their public aliases.
compatibility_note: Breaking compatibility note: section: scopes are intentionally rejected in the owned retrieval service until section fallback support exists. PageIndex and embeddings remain deferred fallback-only plumbing for later retrieval expansion and are not required paths in the current FTS-first MVP; callers that depend on section targeting must switch to vault, collection:, or doc: scopes.
